description: Condensed matter frontiers - topological phases, superconductivity, 2D materials.

facts.truth: only on surfaces while bulk is insulating
distractors: throughout entire volume exactly like normal metallic conductors do
nowhere at all since they are perfect insulators in every region
only at extremely low temperatures very close to absolute zero

facts.context: The fractional quantum Hall effect demonstrates:
distractors: that electrons can be physically split into smaller particles
charge quantization fundamentally breaks down at low temperatures
quantum mechanics fails completely in two-dimensional systems
have been definitively confirmed by multiple independent laboratories worldwide
are theoretically impossible according to BCS superconductivity theory forever
were never seriously proposed by any credentialed researchers in the field

facts.context: Majorana fermions in condensed matter systems:
facts.truth: show promising signatures but debate continues
distractors: have been definitively confirmed beyond any reasonable scientific doubt
have been completely ruled out by reproducible experimental results
cannot possibly exist in any condensed matter system whatsoever

facts.context: Quantum spin liquids are characterized by:
distractors: conventional antiferromagnetic ordering appearing at low temperatures
complete absence of any quantum correlations between spins
superconductivity arising spontaneously from spin fluctuations

facts.context: Twisted bilayer graphene at magic angle exhibits:
distractors: identical electronic properties to ordinary single-layer graphene sheets
no interesting or novel physics phenomena at any twist angle
perfect metallic behavior with no electronic correlation effects
conventional massive electrons as their only charge carriers
no quasiparticles because they are perfect insulators
Cooper pairs exactly like superconducting materials do

facts.truth: remains not fully understood after decades
distractors: is completely explained by conventional BCS electron-phonon pairing theory
has been definitively proven to be pure d-wave spin fluctuations

facts.truth: tunable flat bands and correlated phases
distractors: no changes to electronic structure compared to aligned layers
only geometric patterns with no electronic consequences
perfect conductors without any interaction effects

facts.truth: they may host Majorana zero modes for quantum computing
distractors: they have higher critical temperatures than all other superconductors

facts.context: Strange metal behavior in strongly correlated systems shows:
facts.truth: linear-in-T resistivity violating Fermi liquid theory
distractors: perfect agreement with conventional Fermi liquid predictions
zero resistivity at all temperatures indicating superconductivity
resistivity independent of temperature completely

facts.context: Magnetic skyrmions are interesting for technology because:
facts.truth: they are topologically stable nanoscale spin textures

they are too large for any practical device applications
they have no topological protection against perturbations
